data.task:
Elegance cleanup atom 1 of the Governance-authoring-SSOT continuation: collapse the run-dir read-by-id resolver to ONE owner with ONE name. Delete `localRunDirById` (both overloads + impl, lines ~21-28 of packages/core/src/runner/run-dir.ts) and every `localRunDirById as resolveLocalRunDir` import alias. Keep the real `resolveLocalRunDir(runsRoot, runId): string | null` as the sole resolver.

Complete site set (I grepped the live tree — change ALL of these, do not trust line numbers blindly):
1. packages/core/src/runner/run-dir.ts — delete the `localRunDirById` overload signatures and implementation; keep `resolveLocalRunDir` and `localRunDir` untouched.
2. packages/core/src/runner/index.ts:16 — remove `localRunDirById` from the re-export list (keep `localRunDir`, `resolveLocalRunDir`, `LocalRunIdentity`).
3. packages/core/src/index.ts:319 — remove the `localRunDirById` re-export.
4. packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts:29 — change `localRunDirById as resolveLocalRunDir` to import `resolveLocalRunDir` directly. Call sites at ~223 and ~1070 already pass `{ missing: 'null' }`; the real resolver returns `string | null` natively, so DROP the now-redundant `{ missing: 'null' }` opts arg at those call sites.
5. packages/daemon/src/rundir.ts:6 — same alias fix; drop the `{ missing: 'null' }` opts at call sites ~32-33.
6. packages/daemon/src/oz-context-pointer.ts:2 — same alias fix; drop the `{ missing: 'null' }` opts at call site ~92.
7. packages/cli/src/run.ts:10 and :509 — IMPORTANT NUANCE: line 509 `localRunDirById(runsRoot, resumeFromRunId)` is the ONE caller using the no-opts NON-null form (old behavior: returned `resolved ?? join(runsRoot, runId)`). Replacing with `resolveLocalRunDir` (which returns null on miss) changes the type. Preserve exact behavior: a missing resume dir must still hit the existing catch → print `cannot resume — no pickup brief` → exit(1). Do this explicitly: if `resolveLocalRunDir(...)` returns null, throw/handle so the existing catch fires (do NOT silently `join` a fabricated flat path — the old fallback path would just fail the readFileSync anyway, so explicit null handling is the correct, clearer equivalent). Update the import on line 10 accordingly.

Acceptance / must not break:
- `localRunDirById` no longer appears anywhere in packages/ (verify with grep).
- No remaining `as resolveLocalRunDir` aliases.
- Typecheck passes (pnpm -r typecheck or the repo's typecheck).
- packages/core/tests/run-dir.test.ts stays green (it already imports `resolveLocalRunDir`).
- Full test suite stays green; run the affected daemon/core/cli suites.
- Resume-by-id behavior in cli/run.ts is behaviorally identical for both the hit and miss cases.

Scope guard: write only the 7 files above. Do not touch run-dir migration logic, the legacy flat-dir fallback inside resolveLocalRunDir, or anything owned by ticket 0067. Report the diff and the grep + typecheck + test outputs as evidence.

data.reason: localRunDirById + all aliases gone (grep clean); resolveLocalRunDir is sole resolver; cli/launcher resume-miss preserved via explicit null-throw; typecheck + core(635)/daemon(401)/cli(50) suites green incl. run-dir.test.ts

data.task:
Elegance cleanup atom 2 of the Governance-authoring-SSOT continuation: collapse the queue RECEIPT to one shape. Scope is packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts only (plus any type-only follow-on if typecheck demands it).

Problem: `QueuedAuthoringReceipt` (currently lines ~59-63) is a 4-variant union distinguished only by WHICH id key is present — `{queuedId,reservedTicketId,status}` | `{queuedId,ticketId,status}` | `{queuedId,priorityId,status}` | the bare `{queuedId,status}`. That reads as four shapes for what is one concept.

Change: replace that union with ONE interface:
  export interface QueuedAuthoringReceipt {
    readonly queuedId: string
    readonly status: 'queued'
    readonly reservedTicketId?: string
    readonly ticketId?: string
    readonly priorityId?: string
  }
The implementation signature (currently `Promise<QueuedAuthoringReceipt>` on the impl at ~line 223) returns this single interface. Each branch returns the same field names it does today (ticket-create -> reservedTicketId; ticket-close/repoint -> ticketId; priority-create -> priorityId; reorder -> neither).

MUST KEEP UNCHANGED (these are the designed-once public contract and the wire shape):
- The five `enqueueAuthoring` overload signatures (lines ~218-222). They give each caller a PRECISE per-action return type (route code does `receipt.reservedTicketId`, `receipt.ticketId` expecting a non-optional string). Do NOT delete the overloads and do NOT make those reads optional at the call sites — the overloads must continue to guarantee the relevant id field is present for that action.
- The HTTP/wire bodies: routes/launcher spread `...receipt`, and tests assert exact shapes — `mutations.test.ts:2925` expects `{ ok:true, queued:true, queuedId:'ticket-create-0013', reservedTicketId:'0013', status:'queued' }`. Those must still pass byte-for-byte.
- The loud old-schema-version rejection in `readQueueFile`/`validateBase` (throw on `schemaVersion !== QUEUE_SCHEMA_VERSION`). Keep it exactly.

ALSO CONFIRM (read-only, change only if there is genuine dead cruft — do NOT alter behavior to chase tidiness):
- The `QueuedAuthoringEntry` discriminated union (~line 57) already reads as designed-once on `action`; leave it unless you find a real redundancy.
- `QUEUE_SCHEMA_VERSION` is a single `const = 3` with no leftover v1/v2 migration branches; confirm there is no dead version-handling code. If it's already clean (it should be), leave it.

Acceptance / must not break:
- The 4-variant `QueuedAuthoringReceipt` union is gone; one interface remains (grep the file).
- Overloads intact; no call site in routes.ts/launcher.ts changed and no new `?.`/`as` casts needed there.
- `pnpm -r typecheck` passes.
- Daemon suite green, especially `tests/authoring-queue.test.ts` and `tests/mutations.test.ts` (the wire-shape pins) — run them and show output.

Scope guard: do not touch the drain logic, the enqueue branch logic, validators, or any file outside authoring-queue.ts unless typecheck forces a one-line type re-export. Report the diff + typecheck + daemon test output as evidence.

data.reason: QueuedAuthoringReceipt collapsed 4-variant union -> one interface; overloads/branch returns/wire bodies/schema-version/loud rejection untouched; typecheck clean + daemon 401 green incl authoring-queue + mutations wire pins

data.task:
Elegance cleanup atom 3a (DESIGN — read-only, NO product code changes): design a first-class, verify-gated path to close a ticket through the governed `closeTicket` core op from INSIDE an active run. Motivation: closing tickets 0063/0066/0065 mid-run required a throwaway scratchpad tsx script because today the only mid-run close path is the authoring QUEUE, which drains at a later seam / post-wrap, and the CLI `oz close-ticket` against a live daemon just enqueues. The founder wants closures to commit-and-verify AT THE GATE like an atom, with no scratchpad workaround and no second commit lane.

DELIVERABLE: write a concrete design proposal to `local/runs/cocoder/run_247/design-atom3.md` AND return its full text as your final message. Change NO other files. This is a read-only design atom; do not implement.

The design MUST cover, concretely (cite real files/functions/line ranges you verified in the live tree):
1. OWNER MAP of the current mid-run close surfaces: core `closeTicket` (packages/core/src/tickets/close.ts) and its returned file list; the CLI `oz close-ticket` flow (packages/cli/src/run.ts ~156-200) incl. `closeTicketViaCli` (packages/cli/src/close-ticket.ts) and `closeTicketViaDaemon`; the daemon route that enqueues vs closes; `enqueueAuthoring`/`drainAuthoringQueue` (packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts) and exactly WHEN the queue drains relative to a running atom; `requestReconciliationClose` (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts ~1314) and `closeTicketAfterSuccessfulRun`; the per-atom verify gate + commit sequence the runner uses (find where directive/verify files are consumed and where the atom commit happens); wrap audit and run-history projection that classify commits.
2. THE PROPOSED MECHANISM for an Oscar-initiated, verify-gated in-run ticket close: what the request looks like (a new runner directive kind, e.g. `{"kind":"close-ticket",...}`, vs. extending an existing seam — argue the choice), how Oscar's pass/fail verdict gates it, and at which seam the commit lands. State precisely how it is LEDGERED so wrap audit and run-history do NOT treat it as an out-of-band mystery commit (recordCommitLink / event types — name them).
3. HARD CONSTRAINT (must be satisfied and shown): ADR-0041 'one commit spine, no second commit lane'. The new path MUST reuse governed `closeTicket` + the existing commit spine the atom gate already uses; it must NOT introduce a parallel commitFiles lane. Show the exact reuse.
4. RELATIONSHIP TO THE QUEUE: does this replace the queue-close path or complement it? Recommend one. The async dashboard/founder queue close should remain; this in-loop verify-gated close is for Oscar driving closures during the run. Be explicit about which callers use which.
5. EDGE CASES to preserve: already-closed, missing-open-ticket, stale order.json entry pruning honesty, ticket not belonging to the active priority, commit failure (must surface, not silently strand a half-written governance file).
6. TEST PLAN: which existing suites pin current behavior and must stay green, and the new test(s) that would pin the verify-gated in-run close (name files + the assertion shape).
7. A short ELEGANCE check: minimum new surface area — fewest new concepts/knobs to add this capability.

Acceptance for THIS atom: design-atom3.md exists, is concrete and grounded in real code references, picks ONE recommended mechanism (not a menu), satisfies the one-commit-spine constraint, and names the test plan. No source files changed (read-only). I will evaluate the design at the gate; implementation is the next atom.

data.reason: Read-only design atom; no source changed. design-atom3.md is grounded in real seams I spot-checked (io.ts parseVerification, runCommitGate/commitOnlyScope, directive kinds, unledgeredWindowCommits); picks one mechanism (extend verify artifact w/ ticketClose), reuses closeTicket+runCommitGate (ADR-0041 one spine), ledgers via recordCommitLink so wrap audit won't flag it, complements the queue, covers edge cases, names a concrete test plan

data.task:
Elegance cleanup atom 3b (IMPLEMENT): build the verify-gated in-run governed ticket-close exactly as specified in the verified design at `local/runs/cocoder/run_247/design-atom3.md`. Read that file first; it is the spec and its seam references were verified against live code. Implement the RECOMMENDED mechanism only (extend the verify artifact with an optional `ticketClose`), not a new directive kind.

Concrete implementation:
1. `packages/core/src/runner/io.ts`: extend `Verification` with optional `ticketClose?: { readonly ticketId: string; readonly resolution: string }`. In `parseVerification`, parse it defensively: present only when both fields are non-empty strings; malformed `ticketClose` should throw the same way other malformed verify fields do (keep the existing pass/fail polling semantics intact — do not let a bad ticketClose silently drop).
2. `packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts`: after a `pass` verdict AND the existing atom `runCommitGate` has succeeded (gate first; if it throws, attempt no close), if `verdict.ticketClose` is present: validate (ticket exists, is open, and belongs to the active priority OR is the run's own ticketId), then capture `closeHeadBefore = await git.headSha(worktreePath)`, call core `closeTicket({ ticketsDir: join(worktreePath,'cocoder','tickets'), repoPath: worktreePath, ticketId, runId, committedSha: <atom gate sha>, closedDate: <today ISO>, resolution })`, and commit the returned files through a SECOND `runCommitGate` call with `scope: ['cocoder/tickets/**']`, `commitOnlyScope: true`, `headBefore: closeHeadBefore`, and a `governance: close ticket <id> via run <runId>` message (or the `reconcile ... order entry` message when closeTicket returns closed:false with files). Feed its result through the same `absorbGateResult` used for atom commits.
3. On `fail` with a `ticketClose` present: do NOT close; record `in-run-ticket-close-skipped` `{reason:'verify-fail'}`; the atom stays quarantined/uncommitted as today.
4. Record events exactly as the design lists: `in-run-ticket-close` (real close), `in-run-ticket-order-reconciled` (closed:false + files), `in-run-ticket-close-skipped` (already-closed | missing-open-ticket | priority-mismatch | verify-fail), and `in-run-ticket-close-commit-failed` before rethrowing if the close gate throws.

HARD CONSTRAINTS (these are the gate):
- ADR-0041 one commit spine: reuse `closeTicket` + `runCommitGate` + `recordSuccessfulCommit`/`recordCommitLink` ONLY. Do NOT add a new daemon op, a new directive kind, or any direct `commitFiles` lane from this in-run path.
- The close commit MUST be ledgered via `runCommitGate` so it lands in `store.listCommitLinks(run.id)` and wrap audit (`unledgeredWindowCommits`) does NOT flag it as an out-of-band commit. A test must prove no `run-wrap-bypass-detected` fires for the close.
- Commit failure must fail loudly (rethrow → run goes red), never silently strand a half-written close.
- Do NOT change the existing async daemon/HTTP queue close path (`/workspaces/:id/tickets/:ticketId/close`, `requestReconciliationClose`, `enqueueAuthoring`, `drainAuthoringQueue`) — this complements it.
- The wiring threads `closeTicket`/git/store/event deps into agent-step the same way existing deps are passed (constructor/params object); match the surrounding dependency-injection style, do not import core ticket ops in a way that breaks the runner's existing layering.

Tests (add all five from the design's Test Plan, in `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts`, plus the io parse test in `packages/core/tests/io.test.ts`):
- verify pass closes a ticket through the atom gate → two commit links, standard `commit` events for both shas, `in-run-ticket-close`, closed file exists / open gone / order.json pruned, no `run-wrap-bypass-detected`.
- verify fail does not close → no close commit, ticket open, atom quarantined, skip reason `verify-fail`.
- stale order reconciliation from in-run close is ledgered → one governance commit for order.json, `in-run-ticket-order-reconciled`, no bypass audit.
- priority mismatch skips close → no mutation, reason `priority-mismatch`.
- close commit failure fails loudly → run rejects/fails, `in-run-ticket-close-commit-failed` recorded.
- io.test.ts: `ticketClose` parses; malformed shape rejected.

Acceptance / must not break: `pnpm -r typecheck` clean; FULL suite green — run core + daemon at minimum and show output, especially the new runner.test.ts cases, io.test.ts, authoring-queue.test.ts, and mutations.test.ts (the queue path must stay green). Report the diff + typecheck + test evidence.

data.reason: Verify-artifact ticketClose implemented per design: gate-first then governed closeTicket through a second runCommitGate (commitOnlyScope cocoder/tickets/**), ledgered via recordCommitLink (no commitFiles lane, ADR-0041), loud commit-failure, priority-mismatch guard correct (ticket.priority=owning priority id, verified). Typecheck clean; core 642 green incl all 5 named runner tests + 2 io tests + no-bypass assertion; daemon 401 green (queue path untouched)

data.task:
Documentation atom: update the governed owner-map `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md` section `#### 2. Ticket Close` so it reflects the new in-run verify-gated ticket-close path landed in commit ec529ee (atom 3b). This doc is the SSOT owner map of `closeTicket` consumers; it is currently stale because it predates that path. Edit only this file, match its exact bullet style/voice, and change ONLY section 2 (do not touch sections 1, 3, 4, or others unless strictly needed for accuracy).

Make these precise edits to section 2:
1. Transporters bullet (currently lists auto-close, reconciliation close, close-confirmation, run-action projection, CLI wrapper, Oz chat reconciliation): ADD the new in-run verify-gated close — `packages/core/src/runner/agent-step.ts:handleVerifiedTicketClose`, triggered by Oscar's verify artifact optional `ticketClose` field (parsed in `packages/core/src/runner/io.ts`), which runs ONLY after a `pass` verdict and the atom's own commit gate, and commits the `closeTicket` file list through the shared `runCommitGate` (scope `cocoder/tickets/**`, `commitOnlyScope`) so it is ledgered via `recordCommitLink` and not flagged by wrap audit. Also ADD, for completeness/parity with section 4, the active-run authoring QUEUE close transporter: `enqueueAuthoring`/`drainAuthoringQueue` `action:'ticket-close'` in `packages/daemon/src/authoring-queue.ts` (the async dashboard/founder path during an active run).
2. Edge cases bullet: ADD the in-run-close-specific edges — skip with reason `priority-mismatch` when the requested ticket is open but belongs to neither the active priority nor the run's own ticketId; skip with reason `verify-fail` when a `ticketClose` rides a `fail` verdict; and loud commit failure (`in-run-ticket-close-commit-failed`, run goes red) rather than a silently stranded close.
3. Pinning tests bullet: ADD `packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts` (in-run verify-gated close: pass-closes-through-gate, fail-skips, stale-order reconciliation ledgered, priority-mismatch skip, commit-failure-fails-loudly) and `packages/core/tests/io.test.ts` (verify-artifact `ticketClose` parse + malformed rejection).

Keep the claims accurate and grounded — verify each file/symbol reference exists before writing it (you may grep). Do NOT invent line numbers; the doc uses `file.ts:symbol` style, follow that.

Acceptance: section 2 accurately documents the in-run verify-gated close path and the queue close path; references resolve to real symbols; only `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md` changed; the rest of the doc is untouched. No code/tests change (pure markdown). Report the diff as evidence.

data.reason: Owner-map doc §2 Ticket Close updated accurately: in-run verify-gated close (handleVerifiedTicketClose) + queue close transporters, priority-mismatch/verify-fail/loud-failure edges, and runner.test.ts/io.test.ts pins added. Only the doc changed; all referenced symbols are real (verified in prior atoms); pure markdown, no code/tests affected
